channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court hears allegations of child abuse material and murder charges against singer D4vd
item.description: A court has heard serious allegations against singer D4vd, whose real name is David Anthony Burke, as he faces charges over the death of a 14-year-old girl, Celeste Rivas Hernandez. Prosecutors claim ...

item.title: Farouk Kibet Sponsors Treatment for Kakamega Girl's Swollen Leg in India
item.description: Jennifer Khasoa Barasa, a young girl from Kakamega County, has been living with a swollen leg for 12 years, a condition that started after a groin surgery in 2014. Despite several attempts to seek tre...

item.title: East Africa Plans Mega Oil Refinery in Tanga with Dangote Support
item.description: President Yoweri Museveni of Uganda has shown support for a major new oil refinery to be established in Tanga, Tanzania, which will use crude oil from Uganda, Kenya, South Sudan, and DRC. This initiat...

item.title: Iran Captures Ships in Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions with US
item.description: On April 22, 2026, Iran demonstrated its control over the Strait of Hormuz by seizing two container ships, MSC Francesca and Epaminondas, in a bold naval operation broadcast on Iranian state TV. The i...

item.title: Tammy Haddad on Barbra Streisand’s political views, Donald Trump, and Washington’s star‑studded social scene
item.description: In this Politico video segment, reporter Tammy Haddad examines how Barbra Streisand’s outspoken criticism of Donald Trump fits into the broader political and social climate in Washington, D.C. The pie...

item.title: Mourning black worn by women featured prominently on the first Anzac Day in 1916
item.description: Before the First World War, Australians were already accustomed to mourning black due to high death rates from disease, accidents and infant mortality. Public buildings were often draped in black and ...

item.title: Congress raises concerns over Election Commission's notice to M Kharge
item.description: The Congress party has expressed concerns regarding a notice issued to its senior leader, Mallikarjun Kharge, by the Election Commission. The party has suggested that the notice could be influenced by...

item.title: Tinubu holds meeting with APC governors at Presidential Villa
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu dey currently hold meeting with governors wey dem elect under the All Progressives Congress (APC) at him office inside the Presidential Villa, Abuja. Some of the governors ...

item.title: AAP raises concerns over law and order issues in Delhi under BJP government
item.description: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) has openly criticised the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for the increasingly concerning law and order situation in Delhi. AAP MLA Kuldeep Yadav highlighted that despite Delhi ...

item.title: Picton Investments explores minority stake sale to fund growth and stay competitive
item.description: Toronto-based asset manager Picton Investments has explored selling a stake in its business, seeking potential buyers or partners to support its next phase of growth. The firm, which manages approxima...

item.title: Rivers Police Arrest Three Suspected Kidnappers, Rescue Victim, Foil Another Kidnap Plot
item.description: The Rivers State Police Command has arrested three suspected kidnappers and rescued a victim in a major operation that took place on April 14, 2026, in Etche Local Government Area of Rivers State. The...

item.title: Security Forces Neutralise 22 India-Sponsored Militants in Khyber Operation
item.description: Pakistani security forces and law enforcement agencies carried out an intelligence-led operation in Khyber district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on April 21, successfully neutralising 22 militants reportedly...

item.title: Understanding Freedom Beyond Physical Escape from Townships
item.description: The article reflects on the complex nature of freedom experienced by those leaving South African townships. The author, Lesego Chepape, recounts leaving Tembisa and realising that escaping a challengi...

item.title: To the Moon review: A heartfelt story-driven game that still shines after 15 years
item.description: The article reviews the indie game To the Moon, originally released in 2011, and reflects on how well it still holds up today. Developed by Kan Gao using RPG Maker XP, the game focuses more on storyte...

item.title: Albanese Government Faces Pressure Over NDIS Cuts and Fossil Fuel Support
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's government is under fire for its handling of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and its continued support of the fossil fuel industry. In a striking reve...

item.title: Tinubu Meets APC Governors Following Emergency Talks With Senate Leadership
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu dey currently hold closed-door meeting with governors wey belong to the All Progressives Congress (APC) for State House, Abuja. The meeting wey dem first plan for 4 p.m. la...

item.title: Rueben Bain Selected 15th Overall in NFL Draft Despite Physical and Personal Challenges
item.description: Rueben Bain Jr., a standout defensive end from the Miami Hurricanes, was selected 15th overall by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the 2026 NFL Draft. Despite being a consensus All-American and ACC Defensi...

item.title: Bail Application of Lekompo Artist Shebeshxt Continues at Polokwane Court
item.description: The bail application for Lehlogonolo 'Shebeshxt' Chauke, a well-known and controversial Lekompo musician, continued on Thursday at the Polokwane Magistrate's Court. Shebeshxt, who has previously been ...

item.title: Porsche to Release All-Electric Cayenne Coupe with Multiple Variants
item.description: Porsche has announced that it will launch an all-electric version of its Cayenne coupe in late summer 2026. The Cayenne Coupe Electric, a four-door variant, will join the lineup of Porsche's electric ...

item.title: Coco Martin's First Teleserye Appearance
item.description: Coco Martin, a well-known Filipino actor famously called the 'Primetime King,' began his television career many years ago. His first teleserye marked the start of a remarkable journey in Philippine te...
